note: Minimal replay fixture (anchor case: COVID, the model of doubt). Four coherence-level claims ABOUT the documented evidence base — not verdicts on truth. Provenance carries locators (how to reach a public source), never raw third-party content. The two readers' leans are predefined to exercise all three signals: robust-core, live-crux, unsupported.

claims.text: Phase-3 randomized trials of mRNA COVID-19 vaccines reported efficacy against symptomatic infection in their pre-registered primary endpoints.
The benefit-over-risk balance of routine booster doses for low-risk young adults is settled by the cited evidence base.
Early-2020 school-closure policies were uniformly justified by contemporaneous controlled evidence of their effect on transmission.
Ten-year cardiovascular outcomes following COVID-19 infection are characterized in the cited evidence base.

expectedCoverage.justification: the anglophone peer-reviewed literature is the baseline corpus a scoping review of this dispute is expected to cover (PRISMA-ScR item 8: information sources).
the dispute concerns events centered in China; non-anglophone (esp. Chinese-language) primary sources are pertinent per the WHO-convened global study of origins (joint mission report, 2021).
claims about intervention efficacy are expected to be anchored in controlled trial evidence, the design that addresses confounding for such claims.
